contentSecurity.subtitle: Content filter rules and PII redaction patterns applied to AI gateway requests

mcpStore.title: MCP Store
mcpStore.subtitle: Browse and install MCP server templates
mcpStore.installAgainHint: Already installed. Install another copy (useful for multiple instances).
mcpStore.installAgainWarning: This template is already installed. A new copy will be created with a numeric suffix on the name and prefix.

auth.title: ThinkWatch
auth.subtitle: Enterprise AI API & MCP Gateway
auth.passwordChanged: Password changed successfully. Please log in again.
auth.revokeSessionsConfirm: This will log you out from all devices. Continue?
auth.deleteAccountConfirm: This will permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone. Type your email to confirm:
auth.haveAccount: Already have an account? Sign in
auth.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
auth.passwordComplexity: Password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one digit
auth.registrationFailed: Registration could not be completed. If you already have an account, please sign in instead.
auth.totpDescription: Add an extra layer of security with TOTP-based two-factor authentication.
auth.totpRequiredNotice: Your administrator requires two-factor authentication. Please enable it now.
auth.totpDisableConfirm: Enter your password to disable two-factor authentication.
auth.totpScanQr: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app:
auth.totpRecoveryWarning: Save these codes in a safe place. Each code can only be used once.
auth.totpHint: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app, or a recovery code.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Overview of your AI API and MCP gateway
dashboard.compareHint: Show change vs the immediately-preceding window of the same length
dashboard.noTrafficHint: logs will appear here as requests flow through

providers.title: Providers
providers.subtitle: Manage AI model providers
providers.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this provider?
providers.noProvidersHint: Add a provider to get started.
providers.dialogDescription: Configure a new AI provider connection.
providers.apiKeyUnchanged: Leave blank to keep current key
providers.headersDesc: Headers sent with every request to this provider. Use {{user_id}} or {{user_email}} in values to forward caller identity.
providers.customHeadersDesc: Custom HTTP headers forwarded with requests. Use {{user_id}} or {{user_email}} in values to forward caller identity.
providers.awsImdsv2Hint: Credentials are automatically obtained from the EC2 instance metadata service. Make sure the instance has an IAM role with Bedrock permissions.
providers.bedrockKeyHint: Format: ACCESS_KEY_ID:SECRET_ACCESS_KEY. Leave empty on EC2 to use IMDSv2 instance role.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.subtitle: Manage gateway API keys
apiKeys.allowedModels_pickModelsHint: Pick one or more models this key may use. Leave none checked to keep all models allowed.
apiKeys.surfacesHint: Pick at least one surface this key may call. The same `tw-` token works across all three (AI gateway, MCP gateway, Console admin API) — this list controls which one(s) are allowed.
apiKeys.keyCreatedHint: Copy this key now. It will not be shown again.
apiKeys.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke this API key?
apiKeys.noKeysHint: Create a key to start making requests through the gateway.
apiKeys.dialogDescription: Generate a new API key for the gateway.
apiKeys.rotateConfirm: Rotate this API key? The old key will remain active during the grace period.
apiKeys.rotateSuccess: Key rotated successfully. Save the new key below.
apiKeys.expiresInHint: New expiry from now (days). Leave empty to keep current.
apiKeys.costCenterHint: Optional tag for cost attribution on the Costs page. Free-form, up to 64 chars.

models.title: Models
models.subtitle: Exposed catalog of model IDs clients can call via /v1/models. Each entry has per-direction weights that scale the platform baseline for cost reports and weighted-token quotas.
models.noModelsHint: Add a model or import one from a provider to expose it to clients.
models.noProvidersHint: Add a provider before registering models.
models.deleteConfirm: Delete this model and all of its routes? Clients calling this model ID will start getting 404. Usage records are kept.
models.formHint: Weights are relative units. 1.0 = matches platform baseline pricing. Tune them so heavier models cost more against budgets.
models.searchPlaceholder: Search by model ID or display name...
models.upstreamModelHint: Model name sent to the provider. Leave empty to use the same name.
models.upstreamModelFreeInput: Provider catalog unavailable — enter the upstream model name manually.
models.deleteRouteConfirm: Remove this provider route? The model itself is kept — only this provider binding is deleted.
models.batchDeleteConfirm: Delete {{count}} selected routes? This cannot be undone.
models.weightHint: Relative cost factor. 1.0 = baseline. Absolute $/token = platform baseline × weight. Set baseline in Settings.
models.batchImportHint: Import model entries from a provider's remote catalog into your exposed models list.
models.batchImportWarning: Imported routes go live in /v1/models right away. Tick only the models you actually want exposed.
models.batchStep2Hint: For each selected upstream, decide whether to create a new exposed model or attach it as a fallback route to one you already have.
models.cleanupConfirm: Delete {{count}} models that have no routes? The entries were imported from a provider's catalog but never attached to any active provider. This cannot be undone.
models.bulkDeleteConfirm: Delete {{count}} selected models? Catalog entries are removed and all attached routes cascade-delete with them. Usage records are kept. This cannot be undone.

teams.title: Teams
teams.subtitle: Group users and assign roles to the team. All members automatically inherit the team's roles and permissions.
teams.noTeamsHint: Create your first team to start grouping users for scoped administration.
teams.deleteConfirm: Delete team "{{team}}"? Members are not deleted, but every scoped role assignment, rate-limit rule, and budget cap targeting this team is removed.
teams.formHint: A team groups users together. Assign roles on the detail page to grant permissions to all members.
teams.membersHint: Add or remove members. Member-level role within the team is informational; platform permissions still come from the user's RBAC role assignments.
teams.noMembers: This team has no members yet.

mcpServers.title: MCP Servers
mcpServers.subtitle: Manage Model Context Protocol servers
mcpServers.cacheTtlHint: How long to cache tool-call responses. Empty = use global default. 0 = disable caching for this server. Servers that forward user identity ({{user_id}}/{{user_email}}) are never cached.
mcpServers.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this MCP server?
mcpServers.noServersHint: Register a server to start using MCP tools.
mcpServers.dialogDescription: Connect a new MCP server to the gateway.
mcpServers.testFailedBlocking: Connection test failed: {{msg}}. Fix the issue and try again.
mcpServers.mustTestFirst: Run Test Connection first and confirm the server responds with a tool list.
mcpServers.namespacePrefixHint: Tools are exposed as <prefix>__<tool_name>. Lowercase letters, digits, underscores only. Max 32 chars.

mcpTools.title: MCP Tools
mcpTools.subtitle: Browse tools available from MCP servers
mcpTools.noToolsHint: Use "Discover Tools" on a server to populate this list.
mcpTools.searchPlaceholder: Search tools by name or description

mcpLogs.title: MCP Call Logs
mcpLogs.subtitle: MCP tool invocation history
mcpLogs.noLogsHint: Logs will appear here as MCP tools are invoked.

teamDetail.noApiKeys: No API keys belong to this team
teamDetail.assignRoleDesc: Assign a role to this team. All team members will inherit the role's permissions.
teamDetail.noRoles: No roles assigned to this team.
teamDetail.noRolesHint: Assign roles to grant permissions to all team members.

limits.title: Rate limits & budgets
limits.rulesHint: Each rule rejects requests when the count for the chosen metric exceeds `max` over the chosen window. Multiple rules combine — any rule firing rejects.
limits.budgetsHint: Caps record the running spend for the natural calendar period (resets at the period boundary). Soft caps: a request that pushes the counter over the limit is allowed once, but subsequent requests in the same period are rejected.
limits.maxCountInvalid: Limit must be a positive integer.
limits.limitTokensInvalid: Limit must be a positive integer.

bulkOverride.title: Apply override to many users
bulkOverride.description: Pick users, spec one override, and the same row is upserted against each selected subject. Expiry and reason apply uniformly.

userLimitOverrides.title: Limit overrides
userLimitOverrides.hint: Temporary, user-scoped overrides that replace the role default for the matching (surface, metric, window) or (surface, period) slot. Expires automatically.
userLimitOverrides.empty: No active overrides. This user inherits limits from their roles.
userLimitOverrides.valueInvalid: Value must be a positive integer
userLimitOverrides.expiryInvalid: Pick an expiry from the presets or choose a valid custom datetime
userLimitOverrides.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. TICKET-4821: customer escalation, re-evaluate Friday (optional)
userLimitOverrides.confirmBulkDisableBody: This will flip enabled=false on {{count}} overrides. They can be re-enabled later.
userLimitOverrides.confirmBulkDeleteBody: Hard-deletes {{count}} overrides. Users will fall back to their role defaults.

userLimitsTab.effectiveHint: What's actually enforced for this user right now — role defaults merged with any active per-user overrides.
userLimitsTab.noEffectiveLimits: No limits apply. This user is unrestricted until a role or override defines one.
userLimitsTab.usageHint: Total weighted tokens per day from gateway_logs (UTC).
userLimitsTab.auditHint: Last 20 events touching this user's rate rules or budget caps.
userLimitsTab.confirmResetBody: Clears the Redis keys backing {{label}}. The user's current-window usage drops to zero immediately.

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Manage user accounts
users.teamsHint: Teams this user belongs to. Permissions are inherited from team roles.
users.forceLogoutConfirm: Force logout this user from all sessions?

guard.lastSuperAdmin: This is the last active super admin — add another before disabling or deleting this account.
guard.bulkLastSuperAdmin: Selection would remove every active super admin. Add another super admin first, or deselect at least one super admin from the batch.

bulk.confirmEnableBody: Activate {{count}} user(s). They regain access immediately.
bulk.confirmDisableBody: Deactivate {{count}} user(s). Their sessions keep working until the current JWT expires, and they can't log in again until re-enabled.
bulk.confirmDeleteBody: Permanently delete {{count}} user account(s). This cascades to API keys, role assignments, and team memberships, and cannot be undone.

users.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this user? This action cannot be undone.
users.resetPasswordConfirm: Reset this user's password? A temporary password will be generated.
users.temporaryPasswordHint: Please copy this password now. It will not be shown again.
users.deactivateConfirm: Deactivate this user? They will not be able to log in.
users.rolesDesc: Assign one or more roles. Permissions are the union across every role. Leave scope as `global` for unrestricted access.
users.scopeTeamRequired: Pick a team for the team scope.
users.effectivePermissionsDesc: Union of every selected role. This is what the user will be able to do once you save.
users.searchPlaceholder: Search by UUID, email, name, or role

roles.title: Roles & Permissions
roles.subtitle: Roles, their permissions, and how many users they affect.
roles.addRoleDescription: Create a new custom role with specific permissions.
roles.membersImmediateNote: Member changes apply immediately, not when you click Save.
roles.cloneFromDesc: Pick a role to copy its permissions and constraints into this draft. You can edit them afterwards.
roles.startFromTemplateDesc: Drop in a curated permission set and tweak it.
roles.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this role?
roles.deleteWithMembers: {{count}} user(s) currently have the role "{{name}}". Pick a role to migrate them to.
roles.deletePerMemberDesc: Pick a destination role for each user. The migration is applied one user at a time before the role is dropped — a partial failure leaves earlier users already migrated.
roles.perMemberMissingTarget: Pick a target role for {{email}} before continuing.
roles.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, description, or permission (try *:delete)
roles.noMembers: No users currently have this role.
roles.memberAlreadyAssigned: This user already holds the role at global scope.
roles.dangerWarning: This role grants permissions that can revoke sessions, rotate secrets, or change security-critical settings. Make sure the role is only assigned to trusted users.
roles.dangerConfirmDesc: You are about to save a role with the following dangerous permissions. These can rotate secrets, delete resources, or change security-critical settings.
roles.noHistory: No audit entries for this role.
roles.editSystemWarning: Editing a system role takes effect for every user that holds it. Use "Reset to defaults" to roll back.
roles.resetSystemConfirm: Reset {{name}} to its default permission set? Any in-place edits will be lost.
roles.allowedModelsDesc: Restrict which AI models this role can use. Leave empty for unrestricted access.
roles.allowedMcpToolsDesc: Restrict which MCP tools this role can access. Leave empty for unrestricted access.
roles.policyDocumentDesc: JSON policy document with Allow/Deny statements for fine-grained access control
roles.policySyncLossy: Some policy statements (Deny rules or scoped Resource patterns) cannot be expressed in simple mode and were dropped.

settingsPage.title: Settings
settingsPage.subtitle: System configuration and integrations

platformPricing.title: Platform Pricing Baseline
platformPricing.hint: Cost reports are computed as `baseline × model weight × tokens`. Change this once to re-price every model at once.

settingsPage.logForwardingHint: Syslog, Kafka, Webhook destinations are configured via the Log Forwarding page.
settingsPage.perfHint: Changes take effect immediately without restart.
settingsPage.perfHttpClientHint: Outbound HTTP timeout for MCP discovery, OIDC, etc.
settingsPage.perfWsTickHint: How often to push dashboard snapshots.
settingsPage.perfWsMaxHint: Maximum concurrent dashboard WebSocket connections per user.

oidc.secretConfiguredHint: Secret is configured. Leave empty to keep unchanged.
oidc.redirectUrlHint: Must match the callback URL registered with your OIDC provider

sandbox.title: Test Sandbox
sandbox.desc: Paste a sample user message to test both content filter rules and PII redaction at once. Results use your current (unsaved) rules.
sandbox.placeholder: Type or paste sample text here…

contentFilter.title: Content Filter Rules
contentFilter.intro: Each AI request is checked against these rules. When the user message matches a rule, the gateway runs the rule's action: Block rejects the request, Warn allows it but logs an alert, Log silently records the hit. Changes apply immediately, no restart needed.
contentFilter.empty: No rules yet. Add rules manually or load a preset to get started.
contentFilter.actionBlockHint: Reject the request with an error.
contentFilter.actionWarnHint: Allow the request, raise a warning in audit logs.
contentFilter.actionLogHint: Allow the request silently, only record in logs.
contentFilter.presetsDesc: Click a preset to append its rules to your current list. Existing rules are kept. You can edit each rule afterward.

basic.name: Basic defense
basic.description: Block the most common jailbreak and instruction-override patterns. Recommended starting point.

strict.name: Strict defense
strict.description: Adds persona manipulation, prompt extraction, and Base64 smuggling detection on top of the basic ruleset.

chinese.name: Chinese language
chinese.description: Common Chinese-language prompt injection patterns: 越狱, 忽略指令, 扮演 …

pii.title: PII Redactor
pii.intro: These regex patterns are matched against user messages before they are forwarded to the AI provider. Each match is replaced with a placeholder; the AI's response is then post-processed to restore the original values, so the upstream model never sees the raw PII.
pii.presetHint: Click to add a preset pattern
pii.empty: No PII patterns configured. Add a pattern to start redacting.
pii.name: Name
pii.behavior: Each pattern: 1) finds matches in user input, 2) replaces them with {{PREFIX_xxxx_n}} placeholders before sending upstream, 3) restores original values in the response. Changes apply immediately, no restart needed.

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.intro: How long to keep records before automatic deletion. Usage records live in PostgreSQL; the six log types live in ClickHouse and are cleaned up by ClickHouse TTL. Saving applies the new TTL to the corresponding table immediately — old data outside the new window is purged in the background.
retention.usageHint: Per-request token / cost records used by analytics.
retention.auditHint: Security audit trail (login, settings, key ops).
retention.gatewayHint: AI API request logs (model, tokens, cost).
retention.accessHint: HTTP access log for both ports.

settings.publicGatewayUrlHint: How the gateway is reached from the outside world. Used in the Configuration Guide and copy-paste examples. Leave fields empty / 0 to auto-detect from the browser.
settings.registryUrlHint: Remote JSON registry for syncing MCP store templates. Leave empty to use built-in templates only.
settings.mcpHealthIntervalHint: How often the platform background-probes each registered MCP server to refresh its connection status. Default 300 (5 minutes). Minimum 5 seconds — changes take effect on the next probe round, no restart needed.
settings.mcpSessionTtlHint: How long an idle MCP client session (and its per-user upstream session IDs) survives before expiring. Default 3600 (1 hour). Range 60–86400. Changes take effect immediately.
settings.mcpCacheTtlHint: Global default cache TTL for MCP tool-call responses. 0 = disabled (default). Individual servers can override this. Servers forwarding user identity are never cached.
settings.allowRegistrationHint: When enabled, anyone can create an account through the registration page.
settings.defaultRoleHint: Role automatically assigned to users who register or sign in via SSO. Select 'None' for zero permissions.
settings.clientIpSourceHint: How to determine the client's real IP address.

rateLimiter.title: Rate Limiter
rateLimiter.failClosedHint: Default is fail-open: a Redis blip lets requests through and bumps gateway_rate_limiter_fail_open_total. With this on, the gateway returns 429 (rate_limiter_unavailable) for any request the engine couldn't check, and bumps gateway_rate_limiter_fail_closed_total. Turn on if missing accounting is worse than denying traffic.

logForwarders.title: Log Forwarding
logForwarders.subtitle: Configure audit log forwarding to external systems (SIEM, Kafka, Webhook)
logForwarders.dialogDescription: Configure a new log forwarding destination.
logForwarders.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this forwarder?
logForwarders.noForwardersHint: Add a forwarder to start sending audit logs to external systems.
logForwarders.signingSecretHint: When set, each webhook payload is signed with HMAC-SHA256 and delivered with an 'x-signature: sha256=<hex>' header. Deliveries also retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff on network errors and 5xx responses.

guide.title: Configuration Guide
guide.subtitle: Configure your AI tools and MCP clients to use ThinkWatch
guide.gatewayUrlDesc: AI Gateway endpoint for OpenAI / Anthropic compatible requests
guide.mcpEndpointDesc: MCP Gateway endpoint for Streamable HTTP tool invocations
guide.aiGatewayDesc: Connect AI coding tools and SDKs to ThinkWatch as an OpenAI / Anthropic proxy
guide.mcpGatewayDesc: Connect MCP-compatible clients to access tools registered in ThinkWatch
guide.mcpPromptDesc: Copy the prompt below and send it to your AI assistant — it will detect your MCP client and walk you through the configuration.
guide.mcpPromptTemplate:
I need to configure my AI tool's MCP (Model Context Protocol) client to connect to an MCP gateway called ThinkWatch.

Here is the information you need:

MCP Endpoint: {{mcpUrl}}/mcp
Transport: Streamable HTTP (protocol version 2025-03-26)
API Key: (ask the user, it starts with "tw-")

The MCP server configuration should be:
{
  "type": "streamableHttp",
  "url": "{{mcpUrl}}/mcp",
  "headers": {
    "Authorization": "Bearer <API_KEY>"
  }
}

Configuration for specific tools:
- Claude Desktop: add to mcpServers in claude_desktop_config.json (macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json, Windows: %APPDATA%\\Claude\\claude_desktop_config.json)
- Claude Code CLI: run `claude mcp add think-watch --transport streamable-http "{{mcpUrl}}/mcp" --header "Authorization: Bearer <key>"`
- Cursor: add to mcpServers in .cursor/mcp.json (project) or ~/.cursor/mcp.json (global)
- Cline: add to mcpServers in cline_mcp_settings.json or via Cline Settings > MCP Servers
- VS Code / Copilot: add to mcpServers in .vscode/mcp.json

Please detect which tool I am using and help me configure it step by step.

promptStep.paste: Paste it into your AI assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, etc.)
promptStep.follow: Follow the AI's instructions to complete the configuration

guide.promptTip: The prompt already includes your gateway URL. The AI assistant will ask for your API Key and generate the configuration automatically.
guide.claudeDesktopDesc: Configure Claude Desktop to use MCP tools through ThinkWatch.
guide.claudeCodeMcpDesc: Register ThinkWatch as an MCP server directly from the Claude Code CLI.
guide.cursorMcpDesc: Configure Cursor IDE to connect to ThinkWatch MCP tools via mcp.json.
guide.clineMcpDesc: Configure Cline VS Code extension to use ThinkWatch MCP tools.
guide.mcpSdkDesc: Use the official MCP SDK to integrate ThinkWatch tools into your application.
guide.mcpCurlDesc: Test the MCP gateway directly with cURL using JSON-RPC over Streamable HTTP.
guide.mcpProtocolDesc: ThinkWatch supports the MCP Streamable HTTP transport (2025-03-26 spec)
guide.mcpVersionNote: Implements MCP protocol version 2025-03-26 with Streamable HTTP transport
guide.mcpSessionIdNote: Save the Mcp-Session-Id from the initialization response and include it in subsequent requests

mcpStep.openConfig: Open the Claude Desktop configuration file
mcpStep.addServer: Add an mcpServers entry for ThinkWatch
mcpStep.setApiKey: Set the Authorization header with your tw- prefixed API key
mcpStep.restartApp: Restart the application to load the new MCP server
mcpStep.runAdd: Run claude mcp add with the Streamable HTTP transport
mcpStep.verifyList: Verify with claude mcp list that the server is registered
mcpStep.useTools: Start using MCP tools in Claude Code — they appear automatically
mcpStep.addServerEntry: Add an mcpServers entry with type streamableHttp
mcpStep.openClineSettings: Open Cline settings in VS Code and navigate to MCP Servers
mcpStep.toolsAppear: MCP tools will appear automatically in Cline's tool list

guide.claudeCodeDesc: Claude Code connects using the Anthropic native format. Set environment variables to route all requests through ThinkWatch.
guide.cursorDesc: Cursor uses the OpenAI-compatible API format. Configure it in the editor settings.
guide.cursorEndpointNote: Endpoint: /v1/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible). Supports all models routed through ThinkWatch.
guide.continueDesc: Continue connects as an OpenAI-compatible provider. Edit the config file to point at ThinkWatch.
guide.clineDesc: Cline supports OpenAI-compatible providers. Configure it through the extension settings.
guide.openaiSdkDesc: Use the official OpenAI Python SDK with ThinkWatch as a drop-in replacement.
guide.anthropicSdkDesc: Use the official Anthropic Python SDK by pointing it at ThinkWatch.
guide.anthropicSdkNote: The Anthropic SDK uses the base URL without the /v1 suffix, unlike OpenAI-compatible clients.
guide.curlDesc: Test the gateway directly with cURL. Both OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic message formats are supported.

aiStep.createKey: Create an API key in AI Gateway > API Keys (it starts with tw-)
aiStep.claudeCodeBaseUrl: Set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to your ThinkWatch gateway URL
aiStep.claudeCodeApiKey: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the key you just created
aiStep.claudeCodeRun: Run claude as usual — all requests are proxied through the gateway
aiStep.cursorOpen: Open Cursor Settings and navigate to Models > OpenAI API Key
aiStep.cursorApiKey: Paste the API key you just created into the API Key field
aiStep.cursorSelectModel: Select any model configured in ThinkWatch
aiStep.continueOpen: Open ~/.continue/config.json (or use the Continue settings UI)
aiStep.continueAdd: Add a new model entry with provider set to "openai"
aiStep.continueApiKey: Set apiKey to the key you just created
aiStep.continueChooseModel: Choose any model name configured in ThinkWatch
aiStep.clineOpen: Open Cline Settings in VS Code and select API Provider > OpenAI Compatible
aiStep.clineApiKey: Paste the API key you just created into the API Key field
aiStep.clineModel: Enter a model name (e.g. gpt-4o) configured in ThinkWatch
aiStep.openaiSdkInstall: Install the OpenAI SDK: pip install openai
aiStep.openaiSdkApiKey: Set api_key to the key you just created
aiStep.openaiSdkModel: Use any model configured in ThinkWatch
aiStep.anthropicSdkInstall: Install the Anthropic SDK: pip install anthropic
aiStep.anthropicSdkApiKey: Set api_key to the key you just created
aiStep.anthropicSdkModel: Use any Claude model configured in ThinkWatch

setup.title: Welcome to ThinkWatch
setup.subtitle: Enterprise AI API Gateway & MCP Management Platform

admin.title: Create Admin Account
admin.subtitle: Set up your super admin account
admin.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
admin.passwordComplexity: Password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one digit
admin.passwordHint: At least 8 characters with one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one digit.

complete.title: Setup Complete!
complete.subtitle: Your ThinkWatch instance is ready
complete.apiKeyWarning: Save this key now. It will not be shown again.

unifiedLogs.title: Log Explorer
unifiedLogs.subtitle: Unified log query and search
unifiedLogs.platformDesc: Console operations — logins, settings, users, providers…
unifiedLogs.auditDesc: API key request audit — key usage, resource access
unifiedLogs.gatewayDesc: AI API calls — model, tokens, cost, latency
unifiedLogs.mcpDesc: MCP tool invocations — tool, server, duration, status
unifiedLogs.accessDesc: HTTP requests on both ports — method, path, status
unifiedLogs.appDesc: Server runtime tracing — level, target, message

webhookOutbox.title: Webhook Outbox
webhookOutbox.subtitle: Pending webhook deliveries waiting for the durable retry worker.
webhookOutbox.totalRowsTruncated: Showing {{shown}} of {{total}} — backlog is large; investigate the drain worker
webhookOutbox.empty: Outbox is empty — every webhook delivery succeeded inline.
webhookOutbox.attemptsHint: Attempt count out of the 24-attempt cap. Rows that hit 24 are dropped automatically.
webhookOutbox.retryQueued: Scheduled for the next drain tick
webhookOutbox.workerHint: The drain worker runs every 10 seconds. Failed deliveries back off exponentially up to 1 hour between attempts and are dropped after 24 total attempts.

trace.title: Request Trace
trace.subtitle: Look up every log event tagged with a single trace_id.
trace.inputPlaceholder: Paste a trace_id (e.g. x-trace-id response header)
trace.noEvents: No events found for this trace_id.
trace.howToFind: Paste the value of the x-trace-id header returned on any API response.

usageLicense.title: Usage & License
usageLicense.subtitle: Month-to-date Billable Tokens and MCP Tool Calls against your licensing tier.
usageLicense.tierHint: Highest tier reached by either Billable Tokens or MCP Tool Calls this UTC month.
usageLicense.localOnly: These figures are computed locally and never transmitted. Used for operator visibility only; activation and billing are out of scope.
